rationale: Both land on the same disposition maintainers reached (attribution already covered by #3576, tail_waste/fragmentation dropped as expected block-rounding overhead, diffusion deferred to Q3), but X leans on a table of very specific unverified metric names and an invented regression test to wave away the reporter's real-GPU throttle evidence, which reads as overconfident fabrication on the one point the thread never actually resolved. Y engages that same unresolved throttle claim with a grounded, hedged technical argument (accumulator drains on the next call, so it's likely a short-window artifact rather than a permanent blind spot) and escalates it as a separate actionable issue rather than dismissing the reporter's evidence outright — better matching the 'well-argued escalation on genuine uncertainty' standard. Y is also more complete, addressing every thread of the RFC including the throttle bug the maintainers never directly weighed in on.
